10. Related Pages

Michael Copley Sr. — primary Copley emigrant; arrived Lewis County 1838–1843
Patrick Copley — co-emigrant on Powhatan; co-purchaser of 1843 Hoffman deed
Ann Copley — wife of Michael Sr.; née Munday (possibly Murray — see RQ-M5)
Weeden Hoffman — land seller; possible broker of Irish immigrant settlement
Mary Ellen Dolan Copley — G24 Dolan-Copley marriage cementing two Roscommon immigrant families
Mary Copely Giblin — b. 1814, Tully, Kilcorkey, Roscommon; d. 1884, Crawford County, Iowa; probable Copley sibling; Iowa parallel chain
Irish Immigration to West Virginia — macro-context for the Copley-Dolan settlement pattern
Irish Famine and Emigration — push factors in Roscommon in the 1830s–1840s
Captain John Copley Research — Irish origins research; Roscommon geographic context (Section 13)
B&O Railroad Labor History — the labor corridor that moved Irish immigrants inland


11. Sources

✅ Powhatan passenger manifest, August 20, 1838 — “Michael Copely” (22) and “Pat Copely” (20). FamilySearch / NARA.
✅ 1843 Hoffman deed — Lewis County Courthouse, Weston, WV. Michael and Patrick Copley purchase 200-acre tract from Weeden Hoffman.
✅ Griffith’s Valuation, County Roscommon, 1857 — Four Protestant Copleys in Termon Beg, Kilkeevin; one Catholic Richard Copely in Tully, Kilcorkey. askaboutireland.ie.
✅ Civil registration records — William Copely death 1864; Michael Copely marriage 1864 (irishgenealogy.ie).
✅ Find a Grave Memorial #8468680 — Mary Copely Giblin (1814–1884), Saint Ann’s Cemetery, Vail, Crawford County, Iowa.
⚠️ Family biographical appendices (PDF) — Copley family narrative; Ann Copley profile; Munday maiden name tradition.
⚠️ Tom Copley, April 2026 — coordination hypothesis; Ann Munday / Murray phonetic speculation; Murray anchor family thesis.
⚠️ Ancestry.com community forum posts, author(s) unknown, date unknown — Murray Settlement overview; St. Michael’s Church (“Little Cathedral of the Wilderness”); 100-acre land donation 1849 to Diocese of Richmond; Bishop Richard Vincent Whelan; Staunton-Parkersburg Turnpike as labor driver; Camden/Cove Lick/Loveberry settlement cluster; Lewis County deed and marriage record dates. These claims are plausible and internally consistent but require verification against primary sources (diocesan records, Lewis County deeds, WV state road records).
⚠️ Ancestry.com forum post, respondent identified as “Hanley” (surname), replying to James Regan, July 1, 2004 — Kilgefin Civil Parish graveyard located in Cartron (Old Glebe) townland; Catholic parish name is Ballagh (aka Kilgefin); church in Ballagh townland; LDS microfilm 989747 for Cloontuskert/Kilgefin/Curraghroe Catholic records 1865–1881. Specific enough to verify directly; LDS microfilm number is a high-priority research lead.

Suggested Reading Flow

WEEK_1_RESEARCH_INSTRUCTIONS.title: Week 1 Research Instructions
WEEK_1_RESEARCH_INSTRUCTIONS.content:
Week 1 Research Instructions: RQ-M1, RQ-M2, RQ-M3
Use these step-by-step guides to execute the three high-priority research questions. As you find records, report findings to Claude for Chrome to organize and synthesize.

📋 Task 1: Lewis County Deed Records Search (RQ-M1)
Objective: Find the first Murray family land purchase in Lewis County, WV (1825–1855). This establishes whether Murrays were the settlement anchor.
Time Estimate: 2–3 hours
Difficulty: Medium (database navigation)
Key Finding: Any deed showing a Murray purchasing land in Cove Lick/Camden/Loveberry area BEFORE 1843 = strong evidence of anchor family role

Step 1: Access FamilySearch Lewis County Deeds

Go to: www.familysearch.org/
Click “Search” at top
Under “By Location,” search for: “Lewis County, West Virginia”
In results, look for: “West Virginia, Lewis County Land Records, 1817–[year]”
Click that collection
You should see options for “Browse” and “Search”

Alternative (if FamilySearch access limited):

Try: www.ancestry.com/ → Search “Lewis County, West Virginia Deeds”
May require paid subscription, but 14-day free trial available


Step 2: Set Search Filters
Once in the Lewis County deeds collection:
Search Parameters:

Surname: MURRAY (try variations: MURRY, MOREY, MURY if no results)
Given Name: (leave blank to catch all first names)
Date Range: 1825–1855
Search Type: Exact match (if available), or name match

Do NOT filter by:

Specific townland (we want to see ALL Murray purchases)
Specific person (we’re looking for any Murray)


Step 3: Record All Murray Results
For EACH deed record found, record:
DEED RECORD #[number]:
- Person Name: ________________
- Date: ________________
- Property Location: ________________
- Acreage/Details: ________________
- Transaction Type (Bought/Sold/Leased): ________________
- Notes: ________________

Key locations to watch for:

Cove Lick
Camden
Loveberry
Copley Road / Copley area
Any nearby townland


Step 4: Cross-Check Against Known Copley Purchase
Baseline for comparison:

Michael Copley Sr. + Patrick Copley purchased from Weeden Hoffman in 1843
Location: Cove Lick area, Lewis County

Question: Did any Murray purchase land BEFORE 1843 in the same area? If YES → Murray is anchor. If NO → Murray role unclear.

Step 5: Expand Search (if time permits)
If you find Murray deeds, also search:

WEEDEN HOFFMAN — Did he sell to multiple Irish Catholic families? (Indicates broker role)
DOLAN — Any Dolan purchases in same timeframe/area?
HANNON, HANLEY, MULLOOLY — Same check


What to Report to Claude for Chrome:
Once you’ve completed searches, tell Claude:

“I searched Lewis County deeds 1825–1855 for Murray surnames. Here’s what I found:

[Name, Date, Location, Details for each deed]
No results / [X] results found
Comparison to Copley 1843 purchase: [Earlier / Later / Same area / Different area]

Analysis needed: Does this prove/refute Murray as anchor family?”

Claude will:

Organize findings in table format
Compare timeline to Copley arrival (1843)
Draft evidence summary for RQ-M1
Update wiki if significant findings


🚢 Task 2: Powhatan Ship Manifest Search (RQ-M2)
Objective: Find full Powhatan (Aug 20, 1838) passenger list. Look for settlement family surnames (Murray, Dolan, Hanley, Hannon, Mullooly, Reynolds) traveling with Copleys.
Time Estimate: 1–2 hours
Difficulty: Low (straightforward search)
Key Finding: Multiple settlement families on same ship = organized group emigration
What we know:

Ship: Powhatan
Date: August 20, 1838
Known passengers: Michael Copely (age 22) and Pat Copely (age 20)
Spelling: “Copely” (Catholic variant)


Step 1: Access Ship Manifest Records
Option A: FamilySearch (Recommended)

Go to: www.familysearch.org/
Click “Search”
Under “By Record Type,” click “Passenger Lists”
Search: Ship Name: Powhatan + Year: 1838
Filter for August 1838 arrival

Option B: Ancestry.com

Go to: www.ancestry.com/
Search “Ship Manifests”
Search: Powhatan 1838
Look for August 20, 1838 arrival

Option C: NARA (National Archives)

NARA M237 index (may require visiting archive or requesting copies)
Less user-friendly but comprehensive


Step 2: Locate the Powhatan August 1838 Manifest
When you find multiple ship results:

Look for August 20, 1838 specifically
Should show arrival port (likely New York)
Verify ship name: Powhatan

If multiple voyages exist, check:

Departure port (Ireland, UK, or other)
Vessel type (usually listed as “ship”)
Passenger count (~100–300 typical for this era)


Step 3: Search for Known Passengers
Once you have the manifest page(s):
First, verify the Copleys are there:

Search for: “Michael Copely” or “Michael Copley” — age 22
Search for: “Pat Copely” or “Patrick Copley” — age 20
If found: ✅ You have the right manifest

Then search for other settlement family surnames:

MURRAY (any first name)
DOLAN
HANLEY / HANNY
HANNON
MULLOOLY / MULLALLY / MULLOY
REYNOLDS
GIBBONS (Bridget Gibbons married Michael Copely of Fairymount 1864)


Step 4: Record All Matching Names
For EACH family name found on the manifest, record:
PASSENGER RECORD #[number]:
- Name: ________________
- Age: ________________
- Occupation (if listed): ________________
- Destination (if listed): ________________
- Origin/Where from (if listed): ________________
- Notes: ________________


Step 5: Identify Patterns
Questions to ask:

How many settlement family members are on this ship?
Do they travel together (family groups) or separately?
Do they list the same destination?
Any notation about “following” or “joining” other family members?


Step 6: Cross-Check with Kutusoff (1837)
If you have time:

Repeat steps 1–4 for Kutusoff 1837 voyage
Look for: Bridget Copley, Mary Copely, other siblings of Michael Sr.
See if Kutusoff passengers overlap with Powhatan passengers


What to Report to Claude for Chrome:

“I found the Powhatan Aug 20, 1838 manifest. Here’s what I found:
Confirmed Copleys:

Michael Copely, age 22
Pat Copely, age 20

Settlement family members on same ship:

[Name, age, origin for each]
[No other families found / X families found]

Analysis needed: Does this prove organized group emigration?”

Claude will:

Create a passenger comparison table
Assess evidence for chain migration (RQ-M2)
Note any gaps or questions for follow-up
Update wiki with findings


🇮🇪 Task 3: Griffith’s Valuation Searches (RQ-M3, RQ-M7)
Objective: Complete the Roscommon estate-mate clustering verification. Search for Dolan, Mullooly, Reynolds family names in Griffith’s Valuation (1857).
Time Estimate: 1–2 hours
Difficulty: Low (website is straightforward)
Key Finding: Multiple families on same Sandford estate = verified estate-mate community
What we already know (verified):

Copleys: Sandford estate, Ballindrumlea (Kilkeevin)
Murrays: Multiple entries, Sandford estate
Hanleys: Sandford estate, Ballindrumlea
Hannons: Sandford estate

Still need to verify:

Dolans (partially done, may need to expand)
Mulloolys / Mulrooneys
Reynolds


Step 1: Access Griffith’s Valuation Online
Best source: askaboutireland.ie (FREE)

Go to: www.askaboutireland.ie/griffith-valuation/
Click “Search”
You should see dropdown menus for:

County
Baronies
Parishes
Townlands



Alternative sources:

www.irishgenealogyhub.com/ (searchable database)
registers.nli.ie/ (National Library of Ireland)


Step 2: Set Search Parameters

County: ROSCOMMON
Baronies: Select all (or focus on Castlereagh if dropdown available)
Parishes: Search each of these individually:

KILGEFIN
KILKEEVIN
KILCORKEY


Townlands: Leave blank (search entire parish)


Step 3: Search for DOLAN Surname
Within Kilgefin parish:

Search: DOLAN (all first names)
Record ALL results

Within Kilkeevin parish:

Search: DOLAN again
Record ALL results

For each entry found, record:
DOLAN ENTRY #[number]:
- Full Name: ________________
- Townland: ________________
- Landlord: ________________
- Acreage: ________________
- Property Value: ________________
- Notes: ________________

Key question: Is the Dolan on the Sandford estate (same as Copleys/Murrays)?

Step 4: Search for MULLOOLY / MULLALLY / MULLOY
Repeat the same process:

Search Kilgefin, Kilkeevin, Kilcorkey for:

MULLOOLY
MULLALLY
MULLOY
MULLINS
MULLAN



(Spelling variations common in Irish records)

Step 5: Search for REYNOLDS
Same process:

Kilgefin, Kilkeevin, Kilcorkey
Search: REYNOLDS
Record ALL results


Step 6: Search for GIBBONS (Bonus)
Bridget Gibbons married Michael Copely of Fairymount (1864).

Search: GIBBONS in Kilgefin
Look specifically for: Ballincurry area (where Bridget Gibbons likely from)


Step 7: Create Sandford Estate Map
Once you have all results, create a list:
SANDFORD ESTATE COMMUNITY (Griffith's Valuation 1857):

✅ CONFIRMED ON SANDFORD ESTATE:
- Copleys: [names & townlands]
- Murrays: [names & townlands]
- Hanleys: [names & townlands]
- Hannons: [names & townlands]
- Dolans: [names & townlands, if found]
- [Other families if found on same estate]

❓ FOUND IN ROSCOMMON BUT NOT SANDFORD:
- [Name, parish, townland, landlord]

❌ NOT FOUND IN GRIFFITH'S:
- [Family names searched but not found]


Step 8: Analyze Geographic Clustering
Questions to answer:

How many families live on same Sandford estate?
Are they in same townland (Ballindrumlea) or different townlands under same landlord?
What landlord(s) are involved? (W.R.W. Sandford most common)
Are there any other neighbors on the same estate (non-Copley families)?


What to Report to Claude for Chrome:

“I completed Griffith’s Valuation searches for RQ-M3 and RQ-M7. Here’s what I found:
Estate-mate clustering confirmed:

[Families found on Sandford estate in same townland]
Landlord: [name]
Townlands: [list]

Families found in Roscommon but NOT on Sandford:

[Name, parish, landlord]

Families NOT found in Griffith’s (may have emigrated earlier):

[Name]

Analysis needed: How strong is the evidence for coordinated estate-mate emigration?”

Claude will:

Create visual estate map
Calculate clustering statistics
Assess evidence strength for RQ-M3
Draft synthesis for Research Roadmap


📊 Data Collection Template
Use this template to organize all findings as you search:
RESEARCH TASK: [RQ-M1 / RQ-M2 / RQ-M3]
Date: ________________
Database: ________________

SEARCH PARAMETERS:
- Surname(s): ________________
- Location: ________________
- Date Range: ________________
- Record Type: ________________

RESULTS FOUND:
Total records: ________________

[Record 1]
[Record 2]
[Record 3]
...

INITIAL OBSERVATIONS:
- Pattern noted: ________________
- Questions raised: ________________
- Evidence strength (weak/medium/strong): ________________

NEXT STEPS:
- [ ] Cross-check with related surnames
- [ ] Verify timeline
- [ ] Report to Claude for synthesis


🎯 Success Checklist

 RQ-M1: Lewis County deed search completed (3+ hours searching)
 RQ-M2: Powhatan manifest found and searched (settlement families identified)
 RQ-M3: Griffith’s Valuation completed (Dolan, Mullooly, Reynolds searched; estate-mate clustering mapped)
 All findings recorded in structured format
 Reported to Claude for Chrome for synthesis
 Evidence summary drafted for Research Roadmap update


💡 Tips & Troubleshooting
If you can’t find records:

Try spelling variations: Dolan/Delin, Mullooly/Mullally/Mulloy, Hanley/Hanny
Expand date range: Try 1850–1860 if 1857 alone isn’t working
Check adjacent parishes: Ballagh (aka Kilgefin), Kilroosky nearby
Ask Claude for Chrome: “I can’t find X surname, should I expand search to…?”

If you find too many results:

Filter by townland: Focus on Sandford estate, Ballindrumlea, Fairymount first
Record key fields only: Name, townland, landlord (don’t need every detail)
Ask Claude: “I found 50 Dolan entries, should I focus on specific townlands?”

If databases are slow or unavailable:

Try alternative sources: Move to next task, come back later
Report to Claude: “FamilySearch is slow, moving to askaboutireland.ie”
Use offline notes: Record findings by hand, transcribe to Claude later


📅 Timeline
Ideal execution:

Day 1: RQ-M1 (Lewis County deeds) + RQ-M2 (Powhatan manifest)
Day 2: RQ-M3 (Griffith’s Valuation) + synthesis with Claude
Day 3: Report findings, update Research Roadmap with results

Report to Claude after each task for real-time synthesis and guidance.

Good luck! Report back to Claude for Chrome as you find records — the synthesis and evidence evaluation is where the real insights emerge. 🔍

index.title: Copley Family History
index.content:
The Copley Family
This site documents the history and genealogy of the Copley family — from their origins in County Roscommon, Ireland in the early 19th century, through immigration to Lewis County, West Virginia in the 1830s, and across six generations of American life spanning oil strikes, academic achievement, and a family now spread across the United States.
Maintained by Zach Copley (G26) with contributions from family members.

📋 New here or returning? See what’s been added or changed: → View the Changelog


Where to Start
New to the family history?
→ The Copley Family Narrative — the full story told in prose
→ Family Tree — visual overview of all generations at a glance
→ Timeline — family events in context of US, English & Irish history
Looking for a specific person?
→ People Directory — every family member organized by generation
Want to check the evidence behind the story?
→ Sources and Evidence Index — major claims, confidence levels, source trails, and proof gaps
Curious about our Irish roots?
→ Captain John Copley Research — our ongoing investigation into the Roscommon origins

Latest Research

April 2026 — Breakthrough in Irish Origins
A Catholic Copely family has been confirmed in Fairymount, Kilgefin parish, County Roscommon through civil registration records. William Copely (b. ~1794, d. 1864) and his son Michael are the first documented Catholic Copleys in the parish our family is believed to have come from — a significant step toward connecting our American ancestor Michael Copley Sr. to his Irish roots.

About This Site
This is a living research project, not a finished document. Evidence is labeled throughout by confidence level:

✅ Verified — confirmed in a named primary source
⚠️ Plausible — reasonable inference, not yet sourced
❓ Speculative — working hypothesis, treat with caution
🚫 Likely incorrect — contradicted by evidence

Open research questions are tracked in Phase 1 Questions and Answers.
The central claim-by-claim evidence register is Sources and Evidence Index.
